html:
[3845]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 尼员智 - Name of a Buddhist nun; transliterated as Ní Yuánzhì.
  • 结舍夏夜 - Building a hut during the summer night.
  • 雄大甚悲 - Sound that is both grand and sorrowful.
  • 声不辍 - The sound did not stop.
  • 呜咽涕泪横流 - Intense weeping; sobbing with tears streaming down.
  • 执心正念 - Maintaining a calm and focused mind.
  • 杨元英 - Name of a person; transliterated as Yáng Yuányīng.
  • 太常卿 - High-ranking official in charge of rituals and ceremonies.
  • 开元中亡 - Died during the Kaiyuan era of Emperor Xuanzong of Tang.
  • 削家 - Family or household engaged in carving or engraving work.
  • 圹中劒心 - Sword found in the tomb.
  • 贵人形状衣服 - Appearance and attire of a noble person.
  • 骑白马 - Riding a white horse.
  • 从者五六人 - Five or six followers.
  • 分处家事 - Managing family matters.
  • 汝母在家否 - Asking if the mother is at home.
  • 合葬已十五年 - Buried together for fifteen years.
  • 骑白马衣服如生时 - Riding a white horse and wearing clothes as if alive.
  • 数日须用尽 - Money should be used up within several days.
  • 百年父子 - Long-term relationship between father and son.
  • 诀去 - Taking leave and going.
  • 随骋出上东门 - Following and riding out through the upper eastern gate.
  • 邙山 - Mountain range in Henan Province, China.
  • 市具都尽 - All market goods have disappeared.
  • 纸钱 - Paper money used in Chinese funeral rituals, symbolizing the transfer of wealth to the afterlife.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 广敬寺 - Name of a Buddhist temple; transliterated as Guǎngjìng Sì.
  • 终南山 - Famous mountain range in Shaanxi Province, China, known for its historical and cultural significance.
  • 太常卿 - High-ranking official in charge of rituals and ceremonies.
  • 开元 - Era name of Emperor Xuanzong of Tang, referring to the Kaiyuan era (713-741 CE).
  • 邙山 - Mountain range in Henan Province, China, known for its historical and cultural significance.
  • 纸钱 - Paper money used in Chinese funeral rituals, symbolizing the transfer of wealth to the afterlife.

Proper Nouns

  • 尼员智 - Name of a Buddhist nun; transliterated as Ní Yuánzhì.
  • 杨元英 - Name of a person; transliterated as Yáng Yuányīng.
  • 太常卿 - High-ranking official in charge of rituals and ceremonies.
  • 开元 - Era name of Emperor Xuanzong of Tang, referring to the Kaiyuan era (713-741 CE).
  • 邙山 - Mountain range in Henan Province, China.
  • 广敬寺 - Name of a Buddhist temple; transliterated as Guǎngjìng Sì.
  • 终南山 - Famous mountain range in Shaanxi Province, China, known for its historical and cultural significance.

[3846]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 手如白雪 - Poetic description of a woman's hands, comparing them to snow, suggesting beauty and delicacy.
  • 微挑之 - A subtle form of flirtation or invitation.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 开元 - The reign title of Emperor Xuanzong of Tang, a period known for its cultural and economic prosperity.
  • 长安 - The ancient capital of China during the Tang dynasty, a center of political, cultural, and economic activity.
  • 宫市 - Refers to the markets within the imperial palace, where goods were sold to the court.
  • 东西二市 - The eastern and western markets of Chang'an, major commercial centers in the city.
  • 金光门 - A gate in the outer city walls of Chang'an, historically significant for its role in the city's defense and trade.
  • 书生 - A scholar or student, often used to describe individuals engaged in literary pursuits.
  • 刺 - A formal calling card used in traditional Chinese society to introduce oneself to others, especially in official or social contexts.

Proper Nouns

  • 薛矜 - A person's name, likely a historical or literary figure.
  • 道德里书生 - A reference to a scholar or student from the moral district.
  • 安宜坊书生 - A reference to a scholar or student from the Anyi neighborhood.
  • 裴盛 - A person's name, likely a historical or literary figure.
  • 杨溥 - A person's name, likely a historical or literary figure.
  • 薛直 - A person's name, likely a historical or literary figure.
  • 刘洪 - A person's name, likely a historical or literary figure.

[3847]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 乃邀至外防令矜坐云待糚束 - Elliptical phrase meaning 'then invited to the outer defense, ordered to sit and wait, and prepare something.'
  • 心窃疑怪 - Means 'secretly suspected and wondered,' indicating internal doubt or curiosity.
  • 须臾引入堂中 - Means 'soon led into the hall,' describing a quick action.
  • 其幔是青布 - Means 'the curtain was blue cloth,' describing the material and color of the curtain.
  • 遥见一灯火色微暗 - Means 'saw a dim light from afar,' describing a distant, faint light.
  • 将近又逺 - Means 'approaching and then receding,' describing a movement that comes close and then moves away.
  • 疑非人也 - Means 'suspected it was not a person,' indicating doubt about the nature of what was seen.
  • 然业已求见 - Means 'but had already sought to see,' indicating that the action of seeking to see had already occurred.
  • 见毕当去 - Means 'after seeing, would leave,' indicating the intention to depart after the meeting.
  • 心中恒诵千手观音呪 - Means 'constantly recited the mantra of the Thousand-armed Avalokiteshvara,' describing a repeated religious practice.
  • 至内见坐帐中 - Means 'entered and saw sitting in the tent,' describing the action of entering and seeing someone sitting.
  • 以罗巾蒙首 - Means 'with a silk scarf covering the head,' describing the appearance of the person seen.
  • 矜苦牵曳久之方落 - Means 'struggled to pull it down for a long time,' describing a difficult action.
  • 见妇人面长尺余 - Means 'saw a woman's face over a foot long,' describing an unusual physical feature.
  • 正青色 - Means 'exactly green in color,' describing the color of the face.
  • 有声如狗 - Means 'had a sound like a dog,' describing an unusual sound.
  • 遂絶倒 - Means 'then fainted,' describing the sudden collapse.
  • 从者至其室宇 - Means 'the followers came to their room,' describing the action of the followers arriving.
  • 但见殡宫 - Means 'only saw a funeral hall,' describing what was seen.
  • 矜在其内 - Means 'Jin was inside,' indicating Jin's location.
  • 绝无间隙 - Means 'absolutely no gap,' describing a complete lack of space.
  • 遽推壁倒 - Means 'quickly pushed the wall down,' describing a sudden action.
  • 见矜已死 - Means 'saw Jin already dead,' describing the discovery of Jin's death.
  • 惟心上暖 - Means 'only the heart was warm,' describing a specific sensation.
  • 移就店将息 - Means 'moved to a store to rest,' describing the action of resting in a store.
  • 经月余方苏矣 - Means 'after over a month, finally revived,' describing the recovery after a long period.
  • 东都思恭坊朱七娘者倡妪也 - Means 'Zhu Qiniang of the Siguang Lane in Eastern Capital was a singing woman and an old lady,' describing Zhu Qiniang's identity and background.
  • 王将军素与交通 - Means 'General Wang had always been in contact with her,' describing the relationship between General Wang and Zhu Qiniang.
  • 开元中王遇疾卒已半岁 - Means 'during the Kaiyuan period, General Wang fell ill and died half a year ago,' describing the timeline of General Wang's death.
  • 王忽来朱处久之日暮 - Means 'General Wang suddenly came to Zhu's place and stayed until dusk,' describing General Wang's unexpected visit.
  • 能随至温柔坊宅否 - Means 'can you follow me to the Gentleman's Lane house?' describing a question about following.
  • 朱欲许焉其女弹唱有名不欲母往 - Means 'Zhu wanted to agree, but her daughter was famous for singing and did not want her mother to go,' describing Zhu's hesitation.
  • 将军止此故佳将还有所惮耶 - Means 'General, staying here is good, are you afraid of anything?' describing a question about fear.
  • 不获已至以后骑载去 - Means 'reluctantly went, and was taken away on horseback,' describing a reluctant action.
  • 入院欢洽如故 - Means 'entered the courtyard and was as happy as before,' describing the mood upon entering.
  • 明旦王氏使婢收灵牀被见一妇人在被中 - Means 'the next morning, the Wang family sent a maid to collect the spirit bed and saw a woman in the quilt,' describing the discovery of a woman in the quilt.
  • 遽走还白王氏诸子惊而来视 - Means 'quickly ran back to report to the Wang family, and the sons were surprised and came to see,' describing the reaction of the Wang family.
  • 问其故知亡父所引哀恸久之 - Means 'asked why and knew it was the deceased father who had led them, and they mourned for a long time,' describing the discovery and mourning.
  • 遂送还家焉 - Means 'then sent her back home,' describing the action of sending her home.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 外防 - Refers to an outer defense or perimeter.
  • 糚束 - Refers to something being tied up or prepared.
  • 千手观音呪 - Refers to the mantra of the Thousand-armed Avalokiteshvara, a bodhisattva in Buddhism.
  • 殡宫 - Refers to a funeral hall or place of burial.
  • 开元 - Refers to the reign period of Emperor Xuanzong of Tang (713-741).
  • 东都 - Refers to the Eastern Capital, which was Luoyang during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 思恭坊 - Refers to the Siguang Lane, a place name in Luoyang.
  • 倡妪 - Refers to a singing woman and an old lady, indicating a profession and age.
  • 温柔坊 - Refers to the Gentleman's Lane, another place name in Luoyang.
  • 王将军 - Refers to General Wang, a military figure.
  • 王氏 - Refers to the Wang family, indicating a noble or influential family.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The phrase '乃邀至外防令矜坐云待糚束' is elliptical and requires interpretation. It likely means 'then invited to the outer defense, ordered to sit and wait, and prepare something.'
  • The phrase '须臾引入堂中' describes a quick action but could be interpreted as 'soon led into the hall.'
  • The phrase '其幔是青布' describes the curtain but could be interpreted as 'the curtain was blue cloth.'
  • The phrase '遥见一灯火色微暗' describes a distant, faint light but could be interpreted as 'saw a dim light from afar.'
  • The phrase '将近又逺' describes a movement that comes close and then moves away but could be interpreted as 'approaching and then receding.'
  • The phrase '疑非人也' indicates doubt about the nature of what was seen but could be interpreted as 'suspected it was not a person.'
  • The phrase '然业已求见' indicates that the action of seeking to see had already occurred but could be interpreted as 'but had already sought to see.'
  • The phrase '见毕当去' indicates the intention to depart after the meeting but could be interpreted as 'after seeing, would leave.'
  • The phrase '心中恒诵千手观音呪' describes a repeated religious practice but could be interpreted as 'constantly recited the mantra of the Thousand-armed Avalokiteshvara.'
  • The phrase '至内见坐帐中' describes the action of entering and seeing someone sitting but could be interpreted as 'entered and saw sitting in the tent.'
  • The phrase '以罗巾蒙首' describes the appearance of the person seen but could be interpreted as 'with a silk scarf covering the head.'

  • The phrase '遂絶倒' describes the sudden collapse but could be interpreted as 'then fainted.'
  • The phrase '从者至其室宇' describes the action of the followers arriving but could be interpreted as 'the followers came to their room.'
  • The phrase '但见殡宫' describes what was seen but could be interpreted as 'only saw a funeral hall.'
  • The phrase '矜在其内' indicates Jin's location but could be interpreted as 'Jin was inside.'
  • The phrase '绝无间隙' describes a complete lack of space but could be interpreted as 'absolutely no gap.'
  • The phrase '遽推壁倒' describes a sudden action but could be interpreted as 'quickly pushed the wall down.'
  • The phrase '见矜已死' describes the discovery of Jin's death but could be interpreted as 'saw Jin already dead.'
  • The phrase '惟心上暖' describes a specific sensation but could be interpreted as 'only the heart was warm.'
  • The phrase '移就店将息' describes the action of resting in a store but could be interpreted as 'moved to a store to rest.'
  • The phrase '经月余方苏矣' describes the recovery after a long period but could be interpreted as 'after over a month, finally revived.'

  • The phrase '王将军素与交通' describes the relationship between General Wang and Zhu Qiniang but could be interpreted as 'General Wang had always been in contact with her.'
  • The phrase '开元中王遇疾卒已半岁' describes the timeline of General Wang's death but could be interpreted as 'during the Kaiyuan period, General Wang fell ill and died half a year ago.'
  • The phrase '王忽来朱处久之日暮' describes General Wang's unexpected visit but could be interpreted as 'General Wang suddenly came to Zhu's place and stayed until dusk.'
  • The phrase '能随至温柔坊宅否' describes a question about following but could be interpreted as 'can you follow me to the Gentleman's Lane house?'

  • The phrase '遽走还白王氏诸子惊而来视' describes the reaction of the Wang family but could be interpreted as 'quickly ran back to report to the Wang family, and the sons were surprised and came to see.'
  • The phrase '问其故知亡父所引哀恸久之' describes the discovery and mourning but could be interpreted as 'asked why and knew it was the deceased father who had led them, and they mourned for a long time.'
  • The phrase '遂送还家焉' describes the action of sending her home but could be interpreted as 'then sent her back home.'

[3848]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 爲館陶令 - To serve as the magistrate of Guantao, indicating administrative responsibility.
  • 暴卒 - Sudden death, emphasizing the unexpected nature of the event.
  • 覆破其旱 - To review and reject the drought petition, indicating official scrutiny and dismissal.
  • 胥怨 - Universal resentment, describing widespread anger or dissatisfaction.
  • 長官不死寧有是耶 - A rhetorical question meaning 'If the magistrate were not dead, how could this happen?' expressing disbelief and frustration.
  • 乗白馬 - Riding a white horse, often symbolizing purity or supernatural presence.
  • 旱不慮不成 - The drought will not fail to be resolved, indicating confidence in the outcome despite the official's dismissal.
  • 沮斯議 - To obstruct this plan, referring to the official's interference.
  • 爲厲矣 - To become a ghost or spirit, suggesting the possibility of supernatural retribution.
  • 一考后暴亡 - Died suddenly after one year of service, indicating a short tenure and abrupt death.
  • 庭絶吊客 - No mourners came to the courtyard, describing the lack of attendance at the funeral.
  • 無恩 - Without kindness, indicating harsh or unfeeling behavior.
  • 性清婞 - Nature is pure and resolute, describing a character trait that is both morally upright and stubborn.
  • 妻子不免饥寒 - Wife and children are not spared from hunger and cold, highlighting the suffering of his family.
  • 典吏皆被其毒 - All officials under him were subjected to his harshness, indicating widespread mistreatment.
  • 自喜 - Self-satisfied, describing a sense of self-importance.
  • 素所畏惧 - Usually feared, indicating the officials' fear of him.
  • 战惧股栗 - Trembling with fear, describing intense fear and anxiety.
  • 使召丞及簿尉 - Sent to summon the county magistrate and the clerks, indicating the summoning of higher officials.
  • 诃怒 - Scolded angrily, describing harsh verbal abuse.
  • 颠仆无气 - Collapsed and lost consciousness, indicating physical collapse due to shock.
  • 但通物数无忧不活 - Just report the number of items, and there will be no worry about survival, indicating a condition for revival.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 开元 - Reign period of Emperor Xuanzong of Tang (713-741 CE), a time of prosperity and cultural flourishing.
  • 馆陶 - Guantao County, a place in Hebei Province, China.
  • 岐阳 - Qiyang, a place in Shaanxi Province, China.
  • 严酷刚鸷 - Describes a person who is stern, harsh, and fierce, reflecting a common character type in classical literature.
  • 妻子 - Wife and children, a common term for family members.
  • 典吏 - Lower-ranking officials, often clerks or assistants in government offices.
  • 丞尉 - County magistrate and clerks, higher-ranking officials in the local administration.

Proper Nouns

  • 李光逺 - The name of the magistrate who served during the Kaiyuan period.
  • 馆陶 - The name of the county where Li Guangyuan served.
  • 李霸 - The name of the magistrate who served in Qiyang.
  • 岐阳 - The name of the place where Li Ba served.

[3849]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 谓两衙典吾素厚于汝何故亦同众人 - Direct speech passage with a rhetorical question: 'I have always been kind to you, why do you also treat me like everyone else?'
  • 唯杀汝一身亦复何益 - Archaic way of expressing 'What good would that do?'
  • 当令两家马死爲验 - Formulaic expression used to indicate a test or verification.
  • 遂人遗两疋细马马复如故 - Suggests a miraculous or supernatural event.
  • 我虽素清今已死谢诸君可能不惠涓滴乎 - Humble request, typical of classical Chinese rhetoric.
  • 卒以五疋绢毕指令某官出车某出骑某吏等修违者必死 - Formal command with severe consequences.
  • 一更后方散后日处分悉便家人便引道 - Sequence of events in a ritual or journey.
  • 每至祭所留下歆飨飨毕又上马去 - Ritual or spiritual practice involving offerings to spirits.
  • 凡十余里已及郊外遂不见 - Suggests a supernatural or miraculous departure.
  • 至夜停车骑妻子欲哭棺中语云吾在此汝等困弊无用哭也 - Ghostly or spiritual apparition speaking from a coffin.
  • 每至宿处皆不令哭行数百里忽谓子曰今夜可无寐有人欲盗好马宜预爲防 - Includes a warning about a potential theft, typical of classical narrative structure.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 衙典 - Local official or magistrate.
  • 一更 - A time division in the night used in ancient China.
  • 祭所 - Place of offering or worship.
  • 细马 - A fine or well-bred horse.
  • 五疋绢 - Five bolts of silk, a form of currency or tribute.
  • 修违者必死 - Severe punishment for disobedience, indicating the authority of the speaker.

Proper Nouns

  • 霸家 - The family of Ba (a surname or clan).
  • 岐阳 - A place name, possibly a city or region.
  • 某官 - Placeholder for an unspecified official.
  • 某吏 - Placeholder for an unspecified official.
  • 细马 - Term for a fine horse.
  • 绢 - Silk, a common form of currency or tribute.

[3850]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 家人逺渉困弊 - Family members who travel far and are exhausted and in poor condition.
  • 不依约束 - Not following constraints or not adhering to rules.
  • 吾令防盗 - I ordered the thief to guard or I instructed the guard to prevent theft.
  • 贪寐 - To sleep greedily or to sleep excessively.
  • 马终不失也 - The horse will not be lost in the end or the horse will eventually be found.
  • 近店东有路向南 - Near the eastern side of the store, there is a road heading south.
  • 可遵此行十余里有藂林马系在林下 - You can follow this road for more than ten miles, and there will be a thicket of trees where the horse is tied under the trees.
  • 往取如言得之 - Go and take it as said, and you will get it.
  • 及至都亲族闻其异竞来吊慰 - When he arrived in the capital, relatives and friends heard of his unusual ability and came to offer condolences and comfort.
  • 朝夕谒请霸棺中皆酬对莫不踖踧观听聚喧家人不堪其烦 - Day and night, they visited and asked questions, and in the coffin, there were responses that no one could understand, causing a crowd to gather and listen, which made the family very annoyed.
  • 客等往来不过欲见我耳 - The guests who come and go are just here to see me.
  • 汝可设防事我欲一见诸亲 - You can set up a defense, and I want to see my relatives once.
  • 其子如言众人于庭伺候久之曰我来矣 - His son did as he said, and the people waited in the courtyard for a long time, and then he said, 'I am here.'
  • 命卷帏忽见霸头大如瓮眼赤睛突瞪视诸客等客莫不颠仆稍稍引去 - He ordered the curtains to be drawn, and suddenly, he saw the head of the ghost as large as a jar, with red eyes and bulging eyeballs, glaring at the guests, who all fell down and gradually left.
  • 霸谓子曰人神道殊屋中非我久居之所速殡野外言讫不见其语遂絶 - The ghost said to his son, 'Human and divine ways are different; this house is not a place for me to stay long. Quickly bury me in the wild. After saying this, he disappeared, and the story ended.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 家人 - Refers to family members.
  • 盗 - Refers to a guard or a thief, in this context, it is more likely a guard.
  • 马 - Refers to a horse, a common symbol of wealth and status in ancient China.
  • 店东 - Refers to the eastern side of a store or inn.
  • 藂林 - Refers to a thicket or dense forest.
  • 棺中皆酬对 - Refers to the phenomenon where the dead respond to questions, a common theme in ghost stories.
  • 吊慰 - Refers to offering condolences and comfort, a traditional Chinese practice.
  • 踖踧 - Refers to a state of anxiety or unease.
  • 设防事 - Refers to setting up a defense or a trap.
  • 野外 - Refers to the wild or open fields, often used in burial contexts.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • This long sentence is a narrative that must be broken down into smaller, more manageable parts to ensure clarity and readability in English. It describes a series of events involving a family, a ghost, and a horse.

Proper Nouns

  • 霸 - The name of the ghost or the deceased person.
  • 其子 - Refers to the son of the deceased person.
  • 客等 - Refers to the guests or visitors.

[3851]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 初过于洛水之南坊市喧喧 - Compound sentence describing the initial scene of noisy streets and markets.
  • 渐至水北闻其过时空中如数千万骑甲兵人马嘈嘈有声 - Compound sentence describing the progression of the event with the sound of countless armored soldiers and horses.
  • 俄而过尽每夜过至于再至于三 - Compound sentence indicating the frequency of the event, passing twice or three times every night.
  • 帝恶之使巫祝禳厌 - Compound sentence indicating the emperor's reaction and the superstitious response of ordering shamans to perform rituals.
  • 每夜于洛水濵设饮食 - Compound sentence describing the ritual of setting up food and drink by the Luo River every night.
  • 尝读北齐书亦有此事 - Compound sentence indicating a historical reference to a similar event in the *Book of Northern Qi*.
  • 百姓竞击铜铁以畏之 - Compound sentence describing the superstitious action of the common people competing to strike copper and iron to ward off the ghost soldiers.
  • 皆不久丧也 - Compound sentence indicating the grim outcome that all of them died soon after.
  • 道德里书生 - Literal translation of 'scholar from Dadao Lane'; may require cultural explanation.
  • 遇贵人部从车马甚盛 - Compound sentence describing the scene of meeting a nobleman with a large entourage of carriages and horses.
  • 呼与语令从后有贵主 - Compound sentence indicating the action of calling the scholar and speaking, instructing him to follow behind where a noble lady was present.
  • 年二十余丰姿绝世 - Compound sentence describing the noble lady as being in her twenties with a beauty that was unparalleled.
  • 与书生语不辍因而南去长夏门 - Compound sentence indicating the interaction of the noble lady speaking to the scholar without stopping and then going south to the Longxia Gate.
  • 遂至龙门入一甲第 - Compound sentence indicating the destination of arriving at Longmen and entering a grand mansion.

  • 我未闻自负不孝之名而欲诛不忠之辈 - Rhetorical statement meaning 'I have not heard of anyone taking on the name of filial impiety to punish disloyal people.'

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '其亲兄岷庄在济源有妹寡居去庄十余里' is elliptical and can be read as 'His elder brother Min lived in Jiyuan, and his sister-in-law, who was widowed, lived about ten miles away from the village.'
  • The sentence '忽见妹还问其由来云为贼所掠言对有理家人不之诘' is complex and can be broken down into 'Suddenly, he saw his sister return and asked her about her journey. She said she was captured by bandits, and her explanation seemed reasonable, so the family did not question her further.'
  • The sentence '娣以乱故恐不相全仓卒将嫁近庄张氏积四五年' can be read as 'His sister-in-law, fearing that they would not be safe due to the chaos, hastily arranged for her daughter to marry a man from the nearby Zhang family, who had been living there for several years.'
  • The phrase '子亦寻死' means 'the son also soon died.'

  • The sentence '初应举就杂文试驾毕夜犹早缄置箧中' describes the sequence of events: the person has just finished the miscellaneous essay test, it is still early, and he seals the test papers in a box.
  • The sentence '却归铺于烛笼下取书帖观览则程试宛在箧中' describes the reversal of events: he returns to his lodging, takes the book scroll to look at it, and realizes the test papers are still in the box.
  • The sentence '计无所出来往于棘闱门外见一老吏询其试事具以实告' describes the person's confusion and his encounter with an old official at the examination hall, who is informed of the situation.
  • The sentence '某能换之然某家贫居兴道里倘换得愿以钱三万见酬' describes the old official offering to exchange the items, but he is poor and lives in the Xingdao district, asking for 30,000 coins as compensation.
  • The sentence '自以钱送诣兴道里欵问久之吏家人出以姓氏质之对曰王父死三日力贫未办周身之具' describes the person going to the Xingdao district with the money, inquiring, and eventually learning that the old official's father had died three days ago, and the family is too poor to afford basic necessities.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The long sentence describing the setting and initial communication between Guo Zhu and Duan Zhiqian can be broken down into: 'During the Yuanhe period, there was a person named Guo Zhu who often served as a magistrate in Wuchang, Hubei, and was friends with Duan Zhiqian from Pei State. The two often talked and lamented that they could not communicate between the living and the dead, and they agreed that the one who died first would come to inform the other. After Duan Zhiqian died, several months passed, and Guo Zhu lived in Huayin. One night, while sitting alone outside, he sighed for a long time and said, 'I heard that Duan Zhiqian is well.'
  • The long sentence describing the supernatural encounter and its aftermath can be broken down into: 'He removed the candle, led the figure inside by the sleeve, and they sat together on the same bed, talking about old times. The figure spoke clearly, warning about sins and merits, saying that it could not be deceived. As the night progressed, Guo Zhu suddenly felt a foul odor coming from the figure. It was unbearable, so he hastily touched it and found that the body was very large and unlike Duan Zhiqian. Guo Zhu, being strong, knew it was a strange phenomenon. He grabbed its sleeve and pressed his body against it, but it was firmly immovable. He covered his nose and lay down. After a while, the figure said it was leaving. Guo Zhu pretended to talk to it, trying to keep it until dawn. The figure became more urgent, saying, 'It is almost dawn. If you don't let me go, misfortune will befall you.' Guo Zhu did not answer, and soon he no longer heard any words. Soon it became dawn, and Guo Zhu saw a foreigner more than seven feet tall, like a corpse that had been dead for several days. In the heat, the stench was unbearable, so he ordered it to be thrown away in the suburbs. Suddenly, several villagers saw it and hurried to look at it. They were shocked and said, 'It is indeed our brother.' He has been missing for several days. Last night he suddenly disappeared. They then took the body away.'

  • 华阴 - Huayin, a county in Shaanxi Province.
  • 胡人 - A foreigner, often used to describe Central Asian or nomadic peoples.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The phrase '董观太原人善隂阳占候之术' introduces the subject, 'Dong Guan, a native of Taiyuan, skilled in the art of divination and astrology.'
  • The clause '唐元和中与僧灵习善偕适吴楚间' describes a journey, 'During the Yuanhe period, he traveled with the monk Ling Xi to the regions of Wu and Chu.'
  • The clause '习道卒观亦归并州宝歴中' indicates a return, 'Ling Xi died on the road, and Dong Guan also returned to Taiyuan during the Baoli period.'
  • The clause '观游汾晋至泥阳郡防于龙兴寺' describes a visit, 'Dong Guan traveled to the Fen River area and reached the county of Niyang, where he stayed at Longxing Temple.'
  • The clause '夜寐辄有胡人十数挈乐持酒歌笑其中旁若无人' describes a recurring event, 'Every night, he would sleep and see a dozen or so barbarians carrying musical instruments, holding wine, singing, and laughing, as if no one were present.'
  • The clause '观虽惧尚不言于寺僧' describes a reaction, 'Dong Guan was afraid but did not tell the monks at the temple.'
  • The clause '子运穷数尽故我得以候子' explains a reason, 'Your fate is at an end, so I can now meet you.'
  • The clause '即牵观袂去榻' describes an action, 'He immediately pulled at his sleeve and led him away from the bed.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 六日例当出巡 - Customary for officials to go on inspection tours on the sixth day of the lunar month.
  • 此日人逢必致灾难 - Superstitious belief that encountering someone on this day brings misfortune.
  • 人见即死 - Hyperbolic expression meaning that seeing someone on this day is extremely unlucky.
  • 见人即病 - Hyperbolic expression meaning that seeing someone on this day will cause illness.
  • 前者八座抱病六旬 - Refers to eight high-ranking officials who have been ill for six decades, emphasizing the severity of their condition.
  • 幽防吏人薄福者众无所得食率常受饿 - Describes low-ranking officials who are poor and often go hungry.
  • 此心不忘咸见斯众暗中陈力必救灾厄 - Suggests that these officials must work hard in secret to prevent disasters.
  • 方义曰晦明路殊偶得相遇每一奉见数日不平 - Mystical statement suggesting that meeting on this day brings about a period of unrest.
  • 后有所言幸于梦寐转经之请天曙为期唯唯而去 - Implies that any further communication should be through dreams or prayers, with the meeting ending at dawn.
  • 及明因召行敬僧念金刚经四十九遍 - Describes the act of summoning a monk to recite the Diamond Sutra 49 times.
  • 毕梦曰本请一七数又六之累计其功食天防矣 - Mystical statement indicating that the recitation has completed a cycle of 49 days (7 weeks) and has accumulated merit.
  • 贵人有难当先奉白不尔不来黩也 - Suggests that if a noble person is in danger, they should be informed first, otherwise, they will not come and defile the place.
  • 泛祭之请记无忘焉 - Emphasizes the importance of remembering the general offerings to all spirits.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 薄福者 - Describes people with little fortune or luck.
  • 泛祭 - General offerings made to a wide range of spirits.
  • 金刚经 - The Diamond Sutra, a significant Buddhist scripture.
